text:
With Advance Dining Reservations open 180 days in advance, it can be tough to get a reservation at your favorite restaurants at popular times, and you have your eye on some very popular restaurants. I would call back the Disney Dining Reservation line periodically. Sometimes you can take advantage of a cancellation. Also, let the reservationist know if you can eat early or late. Often, such flexibility can be a great help with snagging a hard-to-get reservation. If you are feeling lucky, you can always try to get a walk-up reservation while at the Park if any last-minute reservations opened up. But in the meantime, to make sure you secure some reservations, I would check out
this list of Disney World restaurants, which you can sort and filter by food type, location, and experience. As soon as you find some interesting or appealing choices, make sure the restaurant participates in your dining plan and then make the reservation.

Hi, Jean! If it were up to me, I'd choose lunch, but that's mostly because my girls are not big breakfast eaters. It hardly seems worth it to me to pay such a big amount of money for breakfast, when they usually only want a bowl of cereal or a bagel at home. We get more bang for our buck at any meal but breakfast.
Having said that, with Cinderella's Royal Table, you sometimes don't have a choice. When you call in for your reservation, ask for the meal you want, but be prepared to maybe have to pick a different meal.
You can check the menus out for both meals at
www.allears.net by clicking on the Dining tab at the top of the page. That will help you make the decision that's right for your family.
Good luck getting the reservation you want!

Hi there, Gloria!
Welcome back to planDisney and thanks for coming by with your question! I see that you're currently visiting the Disneyland Resort, so I hope that you'll see this answer in time!
If you're trying to use a Disney Gift Card to make purchases (whether it be
mobile food orders or
Merchandise Mobile Checkout) in the
Disneyland Mobile App, you'll see an option to "Use a Different Payment Method" at the bottom of the Checkout Screen. If you select that option, it will create a checkbox for you to use a Disney Gift Card. Once you pick that, you'll have to manually enter the 16 Digit Card Number or scan the bar code with your phone's camera. Disney Gift Cards cannot be stored in the App, so I recommend storing the card number in a separate Notes app so you can easily copy and paste it each time you need it.
If you have multiple Gift Cards and don't want to carry them all around, did you know that you can consolidate them into one Card (up to a maximum of $1000) at
disneygiftcard.com? It's a really handy feature to keep track of all your Gift Cards. The site is not a mobile wallet, however, so keep that physical card because you'll still need it!
Have you been enjoying the
Disney California Adventure Food & Wine Festival that's going on right now? I hope that you've found some favorite dishes that you've been able to try. I'm really looking forward to getting that Mickey-shaped Macaron made with SNICKERS bar pieces for myself (although I should probably share it with someone)!
Gloria, I hope that you found this helpful. Please come by again if you need anything further because we'd love to hear from you in the future. You're always welcome at planDisney!
Wishing You a Great, Big, Beautiful Tomorrow!

Thanks so much for sending this great question; we get asked that very thing often!
For guests with dining reservations at any restaurant within a
Walt Disney World park, admission will be needed. However, if your neighbor's reservation for a wonderful
character dining experience is located within a Walt Disney World Resort hotel, then no ticket will be needed at that time.
There are so many exciting character dining experiences, and enjoying one at the most magical time of the year will make your neighbors' night even better! Be sure to tell them to check out this excellent post regarding
Holidays at Walt Disney World, too. There is so much more to see and do during this very special time of year, so while your neighbors are heading to their character dinner, they should be on the lookout for extra-special sights and sounds throughout the parks and resorts. Truly, a visit to Walt Disney World at Christmas is as magical as it can get.
Send your neighbor over to us at
Disney Parks Moms Panel for more answers, Nancy. Of course, come back here for information when you're ready to plan your OWN trip to Walt Disney World, too. We love to help!

Hi Ben! Thanks for visiting planDisney.
Congratulations on becoming
Magic Key holders. I recently bought another Magic Key myself because I love having more access to the parks, great
discounts, and fun perks. I'm looking forward to using my shiny new Magic Key during the upcoming
Holidays at the Disneyland Resort. There will be so much to explore! I've checked my
Blockout dates and availability and made my theme park reservations so I'm ready to go. Terms are subject to change.
Unfortunately, you should be asked to present a valid government-issued photo ID at first use of your Magic Key. Going forward, you'll probably be identified by the photo the friendly Cast Member snaps of you on that first visit, but the ability to show an ID is especially important that first time. If you selected an Imagine Key, your ID will also need to prove residence in an eligible ZIP Code. There are no other forms of proof currently accepted.
It's good news that your wife has already renewed her license. Hopefully, it is on its way and you won't have long to wait. Also, your year of magic won't start until you scan into the parks for the first time, which will activate your Magic Key. So, while you wait for the new license to arrive, you're not losing days on your Magic Key year of fun. I hope that's good news!
If you're not following the official
Disneyland Magic Key blog, do so! It's got great information for Magic Key holders. I always check to see what I can expect prior to my visit.
Have a magical day.

Welcome aboard, Dona!
I’m so happy to connect with a fellow Virginian and talk about
Disney Cruise Line together. Thanks for bringing your thoughtful question our way as you get organized for a wonderful sailing next year - no matter the destination or ship, you’re going to have an amazing adventure! It’s my pleasure to help you understand what bed configurations may be available for your family's comfortable sailing.
There are so many beautiful kinds of families and travel groups onboard, but there is sure to be a stateroom that suits you perfectly. A “deluxe” description can apply to rooms in the
Inside, Oceanview, and Verandah categories but there are some similarities. Please know that there are wheelchair-accessible staterooms which may also have “Deluxe” in their description, and they offer additional space and a different layout. Within each category, there are rooms that have a
capacity for three, four, or five Guests. You will see the specific capacity listed either in your email confirmation or the description of your stateroom within your reservation online. My family of three is often placed in a room without a bunk bed option, as we don't require the sleeping room for a fourth person. Please note that when you make your reservation, the website would only show you rooms that fit your travel party size.
No matter the size of your group, please know that the bunk beds are not open and visible all day. When you first enter your stateroom it will have a bed, a sofa, and perhaps an armchair.
Your incredible Stateroom Host will magically transform the room each evening while you’re at dinner into the proper configuration for sleeping by pulling down the bunkbed (if needed, and if the room allows) or by changing the sofa and armchair into beds. If you don’t need this extra space and have a smaller travel party, you will never see the bunk bed.
The next day your Stateroom Host will transform the room back into “living room mode” just as Mary Poppins cleaned up the nursery and you’ll never see the bunk bed, even if you needed it overnight. This process is seamless to you and will support your family’s sleeping needs without being disruptive to your living space during the day.
I suggest you
call Disney Cruise Line if you’d like to know the exact configuration of your stateroom, Dona. (If you
book a special guaranteed room category you won’t know the exact stateroom number until much closer to sailing.) If you have a little one, you may also request a pack-n-play or bedrails on your reservation under Special Requests > Child Amenities or request them over the phone if there's a concern with bunk beds for a younger traveler.
I hope this helps you understand what to expect in your comfortable stateroom next year! Our Stateroom Hosts are some of the most caring, kind, generous people with a heart for genuine service and have made for special memories with my family onboard Disney cruise ships. I know yours will take care of you with the bedding arrangements for a practically perfect voyage at sea.
Please let us know what other questions you have as you get ready for this special vacation; we'd love to help!
Wishing you magical dreams until your cruise,

Welcome back to planDisney, Cali! It's always such a delight to see your name pop up, and I'm absolutely thrilled to help with your question today! Traveling with a big group means tons of fun and unforgettable moments, and I can't wait for you and your entire party to have an incredible time together at
The Most Magical Place on Earth!
Cali, you're in for a real treat when you dine at
Sebastian's Bistro. I can't recommend the Oven-Roasted Chicken with Tamarind Barbecue and the House-Made Coconut-Pineapple Bread Pudding with Caramel Sauce and Vanilla Ice Cream enough! They're so delicious and packed with flavor—you're going to love every bite!
Now, as you probably already know, it's not possible to book a table for 30 through the
My Disney Experience website or app right now. But don't worry, you've got options! A great and easy choice is to book two separate tables for 15 guests each since the
dining online reservation limit is 20. If you can grab similar check-in times, this could work out perfectly! I know it can be a bit tricky to find two reservations for such a large group, so another option is to give Disney a call at (407) 939-5277. The fantastic Cast Members there will be more than happy to help you out and figure out if there's availability and how that could work for your party.
While it might be tough to get all thirty of you seated at one big table, I'm sure the restaurant staff will do their best to keep you all close together.
I hope this makes your planning a little easier, Cali! If you have any more questions or need anything else,
feel free to reach out anytime. We're always here, excited to help our wonderful friends like you!

Wow - a surprise Christmas vacation! I know your Pirates and Princess will be thrilled with this surprise vacation. I think they will really enjoy a day at Disney's Hollywood Studios. There are many attractions that will make them happy.
One of my favorite roller coasters in located in this theme park - Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring Aerosmith is a hit and this roller coaster is so fast! Star Tours is a must see attraction especially if they are into Star Wars. The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror is a "dropping" attraction that they must not miss! There are also a few shows that they must see - Lights, Motors, Action! Extreme Stunt Show and Indiana Jones Epic Stunt Spectacular!
I know there are many attractions in this park that will definitely keep them busy for a day. Please go HERE to view the attractions at this theme park. I hope this helps!
Have a magical day!

Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween Party is one of my favorite special events that Walt Disney World has to offer. You won't want to miss the Boo to You Halloween Parade or HalloWishes, the special fireworks spectacular only seen on party nights.
Be sure that your tickets to the party show up on your My Disney Experience account and this should mean that your tickets are linked to your Magic Bands. However, since the Magic Bands are a fairly new system, it is always safe, in my opinion, to have a hard copy folded up in your backpack for easy reference in case there is a problem encountered. You can never be too safe.
Have a spooky time in this magical place.

I can tell that you are super excited about your upcoming Walt Disney World Resort vacation in September, and I do not blame you! You and your children are going to love the
Disney Junior Play 'n Dine Character Dining at Hollywood & Vine. Though Sheriff Callie is not currently available, the characters that are scheduled to make appearances are Sofia the First, Doc McStuffins, Jake and the Neverland Pirates and Handy Manny.
Please let us know if you have additional questions.

Aloha Patrick, I'm happy to welcome you to planDisney!
There's really nothing like experiencing that special slice of paradise that we call
Disney's Polynesian Village Resort (and frequent fans may affectionately refer to as "the Poly") and it seems to be very popular for folks and families alike seeking those balmy island vibes to
dine on some delicious tropical delights at locations like
'Ohana before sticking around to see the night sky light up with fireworks.
- I've personally frequently taken advantage of the bus service late in the evening and while it may always be subject to availability that can change without prior notice, you should be able to make it back to Disney Springs that night - just like so many locals do while leaving their cars there before having dinner at a Disney Resort Hotel location. That said, we have also experienced times when buses may seemingly never show up or take an extended amount of time beyond their scheduled arrival on the digital signage, so don't hesitate to ask another driver for help just in case you may be stranded. (In the end, it might be a good idea to factor in the cost of scheduling a ride through an app like using Minnie Van Service just in case, though in my opinion you shouldn't need to.)
I hope that this was helpful to you Patrick - with the
EPCOT International Food & Wine Festival in full swing during your visit, I hope that you get a chance to enjoy some incredible Disney Eats and a lot of treats while you're here!
Wishing you the safest and swiftest journeys back after dark!

Hi Isabel! It sounds like you are hoping to plan quite a wonderful 21st birthday party. The
Grand One Yacht offers a one of a kind specialty cruise for Disney guests. You can rent it for one hour or several hours and can have private dining included or just some snacks or light dining.
My family has rented this boat several times and truly loved the experiences each time. As far as how many guests it can hold you will need to go by the Disney website number. Personally, I can't even imagine putting 18 on this wonderful yacht. There is limited outside seating so if you ask a large number of guests to join you many will be settled inside around the table and living area. I strongly suggest you keep your numbers low so everyone can enjoy this experience to the fullest. When they cruise outside of the Magic Kingdom you will all want to gather together outside for the fireworks.
If you are considering food I suggest ordering a great birthday cake with some other sweets such as chocolate covered strawberries and champagne. This makes a great option for a cruise without going over the top. You can also add some simple appetizer choices for a complete serving.
Whatever you decide I am sure it will be a cruise to remember!

Bonjour Jennifer! Be Our Guest Restaurant is a stunning restaurant in New Fantasyland that I highly recommend! I have dined at the popular new dining location twice now and thoroughly enjoyed the ambiance, service, and delicious dishes on both visits! Be Our Guest Restaurant is considered a quick-service location at lunch and a table-service meal at dinner. I am happy to tell you that the amazing dishes are served on china at both lunch and dinner. At lunch, guests order on a touch-screen terminal and the meals will then be magically delivered to the table. Advanced Dining Reservations are only accepted for dinner. Follow this link to learn more about
Be Our Guest Restaurant at Magic Kingdom Park. I hope this information helps! Please don't hesitate to write me back with any additional questions. Best wishes for a
magical trip, Jennifer!

Thanks for stopping by the Disney Parks Moms Panel. Your family will have a wonderful time exploring Walt Disney World Resort! Your
dining reservations should include all Guests in your dining party, including your little one. This will ensure there is plenty of room at the table for your family to comfortable enjoy your meal. I have visited Walt Disney World Resort with both of my sons when they were just tiny Mouseketeers and I found that our meals were much more pleasant and enjoyable when our little ones had plenty of space of their own. It allowed them to feel like a big kid during the meal and it also gave me a break from holding a baby so I was refreshed when we went back into the theme parks.
If you are having trouble getting reservations, I recommend that you keep checking back for openings. As a last-minute planner, I have found that additional reservations often become available as my trip approaches, especially the week of our trip or even the day before our meal. So if I can't find a reservation for exactly what I want, I will make the closest reservation I can find, often at an alternate restaurant, and keep looking for other reservations to open. Also, if you search for reservations for an entire meal (for example Lunch rather than 11:00 a.m.), you will be able to see all available reservations for that meal as opposed to reservations only around your specified time.When you are searching for reservations, you will find meals listed at the top of the menu, above all time slots. This is a great way to search through all openings in a restaurant and it has been very helpful when I have been looking for reservations when not much is available!
Please let us know if you have any other questions as you plan your trip. T-T-F-N, ta ta for now!

Welcome to planDisney, Eunjung! I am so glad you found us to ask your questions about your December vacation to The Most Magical Place on Earth.
When my children were young, I planned every detail of our trips to make things as easy as possible. I had diapers & wipes, extra clothes, snacks, bottles, and basically everything except the kitchen sink in the diaper bag. I also always brought our own stroller, so that I knew my kids would be comfortable.
Guests are permitted to bring strollers to the Walt Disney World Resort as long as they fit within the stroller size guidelines.
Walt Disney World Resort Rules state that strollers must not be larger than 31" (79 cm) x 52" (132 cm) and wagons, including stroller wagons, are not permitted. After some online checking, I found that the Uppababy Vista Stroller measurements do fall within the Walt Disney World guidelines.
Stroller Rentals are also available at all four theme parks as well as Disney Springs, if you prefer to leave your stroller at home. Strollers can be hard to distinguish in a literal ocean of strollers, and you often must leave the stroller parked while you enjoy an attraction. I recommend tying something on the handle that will make your stroller easily identifiable! A Mickey balloon was always my first choice, just don’t forget which color you purchased!
You can also bring a bag or small soft-sided cooler into the theme parks with milk or food for your child. Just know that loose ice isn’t allowed, so to keep things cold, you will need reusable ice packs or baggies of ice. I have also frozen bottles of water in the past to keep things nice and chilled and then we can drink the water later once it is thawed.
I hope you have a fantastic visit to Walt Disney World next month! Please head back to planDisney if you think of any other questions prior to your magical vacation.

Thank you for bringing your question to the Disney Parks Moms Panel!
I was able to see
Disney Movie Magic and
Star Wars: A Galactic Spectacular for the first time last winter. Wow! As someone who knows very little about Star Wars, I was still mesmerized and impressed by the show. Those two nighttime shows run back to back in the same location. I think the best place to stand is somewhere near the center of Grauman’s Chinese Theatre, but far enough back that you can see the whole building.
I am assuming you are talking about
Fantasmic as the third show. Normally, Fantasmic will run during the same time as those two shows. In busier months, there will often be a second Fantasmic, but not usually in February. You can find a list of showtimes on this
link to the Disney's Hollywood Studios calendar.
I hope this helps! Please let me know if you have any other questions.

Hi Mary! Welcome to the Disney Parks Moms Panel. Thank you for stopping by with your question.
My daughter recently experienced her first roller coaster and my heart could not be happier that it was also a Disney first at the Walt Disney World Resort! She was excited yet apprehensive, so I understand wanting to make that first experience is a safe, “warm-up”, for all the later exciting adventures as your little mousketeer grows into the bigger thrill attractions.
Mary, for my daughter, I chose
The Barnstormer at Magic Kingdom Park, a goofy roller coaster, with the perfect amount of thrills for little ones to get their feet wet. It’s a fairly short ride so, if they end up not loving it, it’s over pretty fast and you can try again some other time. Another good choice is Seven Dwarfs Mine Train. It has some fun thrills, but nothing super scary. It also has a fun break in the middle, so they can take a breather and enjoy the attraction at a slower pace for a moment.
I can’t imagine a more appropriate first roller coaster than Slinky Dog Dash, the brand-new family-friendly coaster ride at
Toy Story Land! When you walk past it, it looks scarier than it is. But this family-friendly attraction is a great choice for littles who will likely recognize those beloved Toy Story characters that will welcome them with big smiles for big fun!
Pro-tip! Choose the front row for a less intense experience on coasters. And for more options, browse the attractions by filtering by height so you can read more about each experience and choose the best fit for your little guy.
I hope this visit is one for the books as you check-off that rite of passage. Pixie dust away! ~Emille

How exciting that you're celebrating your son's birthday at Walt Disney World!
Late September is a great time of year to visit - in fact it's one of my
Crowds are generally lower since everyone is back to school and weather
starts to cool down just a little, but still plenty warm enough for some
great swimming and even water park fun!
There are some cool activities you may want to consider for your son. Four different 'Pirate Adventure Cruises' are available for children aged 4-12 and are reserved in advance by calling (407)WDW-DINE. Cruises take place in the morning and are approximately 90-120 minutes long. Dates and times of these adventures differ by location and pricing is approximately $35/child.
If that's not quite enough Pirate for your little guy, a visit to
The Pirates League is sure to be right up his alley! This amazing experience takes place in Magic Kingdom theme park and is great fun for kids and grown-ups alike!
A great, celebratory experience for the whole family is the
Pirates and Pals Fireworks Voyage. It's a fantastic way to view the Magic Kingdom fireworks, have a meet & greet with characters from Disney's Peter Pan, enjoy trivia and prizes and even view the Electric Water Pageant.
Another great must-do's for a birthday boy may include a visit to the Jedi Training Academy at Disney's Hollywood Studios. Arrive early with your little Padawan to sign up as this is a
very popular attraction which fills quickly for the day!
I hope you'll visit me again soon with more great questions to help make this birthday extra special for your son. Wishing you a simply Magical time celebrating at Walt Disney World Alyssa!

Happiest Greetings on Earth, Nadia! Welcome to planDisney and thank you for bringing us such a wonderful question about your upcoming stay during the
merriest season of all!
I'm already so excited for the holidays this year, aren't you? What a special gift to be able to spend them at 'The Happiest Place on Earth!' It really is magical and I hope you and your loved ones make unforgettable memories this year together.
I quickly looked at availability for a partial view room type at
Disney's Grand Californian Hotel & Spa and it does look like there are still some rooms available during your dates, at the time that I'm currently writing. But, availability can change quickly, so if this is on your holiday list, I suggest you check it twice and ensure you've modified your hotel reservation for this room type! You can do this online by logging in to your Disney account and selecting "
My Hotel Reservations." There, you'll be able to see your currently held hotel reservation (be sure to verify the confirmation number) and the option to "Change or Cancel Reservation." Upon selecting this button, the next page will ask you which changes you wish to make and you can choose the hotel or room type. This will open a search tool where you can locate availability for your preferred room or view type and the new price resulting from any change. Alternatively, you can call the
Hotel Reservation line and speak with a friendly Cast Member to make changes to your reservation. I recommend having your confirmation number and the name of the primary Guest on hand for ease of reference during this call.
Nadia, waiting until you arrive on-site to request your room upgrade is technically a possibility but these requests are subject to availability and not guaranteed. I would hate to have you leave it to chance if this is truly your heart's desire. Plus, the holiday season is a popular time of year to visit so it wouldn't surprise me to see room availability quickly book up! I hope this information has been helpful and that you can secure your preferred room type shortly! You're going to love staying at Disney's Grand Californian Hotel & Spa during the winter holidays. One quick tip is to check out the Great Hall Cart in the lobby area which offers holiday and other themed treats. They're often wrapped beautifully and make wonderful souvenirs to take home (or enjoy right away!) My favorite is the Mickey-shaped Gingerbread and I often pack a few to bring home and give out as holiday gifts.
I hope these are some fun, festive, and helpful tips for you, Nadia! Please come back to
planDisney if we can help with anything else in the meantime as you plan your upcoming visit to Disneyland Resort. Wishing you the most magical holidays imaginable!

My family loves
Mickey's Not So Scary Halloween Party. We enjoy the party atmosphere, the special characters, the trick or treating, and having short lines on rides.
My family also enjoys
Pirates and Pals Firework Voyage, including the pre-party snacks, the hilarious boat captain, the character interaction, and the close up view of the fireworks. My boys each won pirate prizes during our last cruise for answering some trivia questions.
If you are not able to enjoy both this vacation, I would slightly lean towards the Mickey's Not So Scary Halloween Party because it will give you and your family more options. The Firework Voyage will be a shorter event and you will spend less time on your feet. But given that the lines for attractions at Mickey's Not So Scary Halloween Party are generally short, you likely won't be standing and waiting on long lines. It seems the Halloween Party will give you the best of both worlds, the ability to stand and move around if you want, but also time to sit and relax if needed. And if for some reason you feel the need to leave, it will be easier to exit the Halloween Party.
Enjoy your vacation.

Apr 16, 2014
Sandra from AL
Note: This answer was provided prior to the change to planDisney and may still contain references to Disney Parks Moms Panel.
Experiences, policies, pricing and other offerings are subject to change and may have changed since the date of this answer.
We are renting. A two bedroom boardwalk villa for a week in May. We rented this through Disney vacation club. Will we have mouse maid service for this villa?
Hi Sandra! Congratulations on your upcoming visit to Walt Disney World Resort. You have a fantastic opportunity to experience the luxurious accommodations of the Disney Vacation Club! The answer to your question depends on how your resort reservation was booked.
If your resort accommodation was booked using Disney Vacation Club points then the answer is NO you will not receive daily "mouse-keeping" instead you'll receive trash and towel service on day 4 for stays of 7 or less nights.
On the 4th day, Cast Members from Housekeeping will empty your trash, provide fresh bathroom linens, replace shampoo, and all soap, replenish facial tissues, paper towels and toilet paper, dishwashing liquid, detergent, sponges, laundry detergent and coffee.
For stays of 8 or more nights, full-cleaning service is provided on day 4 with trash and towel service is provided again on the 8th day.
If your reservation was paid with cash and instead of Disney Vacation Club points then you will have daily housekeeping during your stay.
I hope that helps answer your question. Wishing you a magical and memorable vacation!

Hi there, Elizabeth! I love seeing fellow Washingtonians stop by planDisney with their questions.
It looks like your arrival date was earlier this week so I hope you have already enjoyed a magical visit to the
Happiest Place on Earth, despite some changes in your travel party. Rest assured that you've already done an important step to cancel the theme park reservation previously held by the member of your party who is unable to travel. Now, let's talk about future use of the purchased park ticket.
It sounds like you might already be familiar with making and canceling theme park reservations, but here are some helpful steps. First, I suggest visiting the
Theme Park Tickets & Reservations page to view
theme park reservation availability and
create your party by linking all tickets to your Disney account. If you haven't already done this step already, you'll be asked to enter the ticket ID number for each person in your party and the name of each ticket holder. Then, you can select the ticket for which you'd like to make a theme park reservation on each of your selected dates and follow the prompts to confirm your plans.
Ticket expiration dates and terms will be listed both on the PDF document that contains the tickets themselves, and in the email confirmation you should have received. Expiration dates vary by the type of ticket purchased, so your best bet is to consult the
terms and conditions listed on the eTickets you purchased, or to contact
Guest Services to speak with a cast member in Ticketing. If the guest in your party is unable to travel before their ticket expires, there is also the option to apply the purchase price of a wholly-unused ticket toward a new ticket of equal or greater value.
Elizabeth, I genuinely hope your recent Disneyland Resort visit was absolutely magical, even with any twists and turns that might have come your way. If you ever have any other questions that I or my fellow panelists can help answer, we'd love to see you come by

Hi, Allison! Welcome to planDisney!
Fireworks are one of my favorite parts of any Walt Disney World Resort vacation! I was beyond excited when the
Happily Ever After fireworks spectacular returned to Magic Kingdom Park last spring. Happily Ever After is absolutely breathtaking! The message behind this show is so beautiful, and I am moved to happy tears every time I watch it.
Since I'm not sure exactly which night you and your family will be visiting Magic Kingdom Park for fireworks, I'm happy to give you some advice for a couple of different scenarios. I see that you're visiting in October. On fall nights when Magic Kingdom Park closes early for
Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween Party, Happily Ever After is not performed. Instead, there will be a special Halloween-themed fireworks show during the party called
Disney's Not-So-Spooky Spectacular. If you happen to be attending Mickey's Not-So-Scary Halloween Party and your dinner reservation at
Cinderella's Royal Table is on that night, you should have no problem catching the fireworks show. The fireworks happen a bit later on party nights, and that coupled with the lower crowds at this special event should allow you to grab a good viewing spot after dinner.
If your dinner reservation happens to be on a regular Magic Kingdom evening, things might be a bit trickier. It looks like 8:00 p.m. is the showtime for Happily Ever After on nights that it is being performed in October. Cinderella's Royal Table is a really special character dining experience that your family won't want to rush through. I'm afraid that with a 7:10 p.m. reservation, you may not be finished with dinner in time for Happily Ever After. Even if you leave the restaurant by 8:00 p.m., the pathways to the front of the castle will already be blocked off for the show.
My best advice to you is to try to modify the time of your dining reservation. If an earlier dinner time is available, your family could relax and enjoy their dinner and still have time to find a good viewing spot for Happily Ever After. If you cannot modify your dining reservation, depending on the rest of your vacation itinerary, perhaps you could catch Happily Ever After on a different night. If your theme park tickets include the
Park Hopper option, you could hop to Magic Kingdom Park on another evening. If you do not have Park Hopper tickets, you could even watch the fireworks from the grounds of one of the nearby resort hotels. While you wouldn't be able to see the projections on the castle, the
monorail resorts offer viewing areas where the Happily Ever After soundtrack is piped in. If none of these options work, you could always watch the fireworks from behind Cinderella Castle after your dinner. You would miss out on the lighting aspects of the show, but the area behind the castle is rarely crowded and the view of the fireworks themselves is great.
Allison, I hope everything works out and that you and your family will be able to experience the beauty of a Magic Kingdom Park fireworks spectacular. Please stop by planDisney again if you have any additional questions. Have a magical day!

Welcome to planDisney Lauren! Thanks for reaching out with your question. I'm excited to help you! How wonderful that you're planning a trip to
Walt Disney World Resort! You’re sure to create some amazing memories at The Most Magical Place on Earth!
For Martin Luther King Jr. weekend, which can be moderately busy at Disney World, here's my recommended park schedule for January 16-19 to maximize your experience:
- January 16 (Thursday): Magic Kingdom –Starting with Magic Kingdom on Thursday should provide a great kickoff with manageable crowds, as the weekend surge won’t have fully started yet.
- January 17 (Friday): Disney's Animal Kingdom – Visiting Animal Kingdom on Friday can be a good idea, as it generally handles crowds well and experiences slightly fewer visitors than other parks before the weekend.
- January 18 (Saturday): EPCOT – EPCOT is spacious and can better absorb larger weekend crowds, making it a smart choice for Saturday. Using your Lightning Lane options will help with popular attractions like Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind.
- January 19 (Sunday): Disney's Hollywood Studios – While weekends are busy for Hollywood Studios, Sunday will allow you to use your full set of Lightning Lane passes to manage waits for high demand rides like Rise of the Resistance and Tower of Terror.
This plan balances park capacity and crowd flow, maximizing your experience across the four days.
I hope this information helps you prepare for an exciting visit to Walt Disney World Resort, Lauren! Thank you again for visiting planDisney. We'll see ya real soon!

planDisney! Thank you for stopping by and sharing your question with us!
I love the

Thanks for your question about the Disneyland Resort. I have a 7-year-old son and 2-year-old daughter, so I know what it’s like to navigate the theme parks with two little ones and their differing interests!
Check out the
Attractions list online to make your family's list of must-dos. If there is no minimum height requirement for an attraction, then it’s safe for your daughter to ride! You and your partner may want to take advantage of the
Rider Switch program for the more thrilling attractions your baby can't experience. One of you can ride while the other waits with one or both of your kids. You then "swap" so the second adult can enjoy the attraction without waiting in line all over again. The
Baby Care Centers are another great resource for parents. They provide full-size changing tables, high chairs, private nursing spaces and baby food prepping areas for your convenience.
Be sure to download the official Disneyland Mobile App so you can locate characters in the theme parks by checking the Characters tab. “Minnie & Friends – Breakfast in the Park” at Plaza Inn is a good choice for a yummy buffet of breakfast classics while visiting with Minnie and the gang without waiting in line, since the characters visit your table for photos! Dining reservations can be made online or by calling (714) 781-DINE.
When you arrive, pick up complimentary “1st Visit” celebration buttons at a Guest Services location to add a little extra magic to your vacation. I hope this helps, Anna, and that you have a pixie dust-packed visit to the Happiest Place on Earth with your young Mouseketeers!
Wishing you magic,

What format is the meal plan in. I have purchased one for myself, my husband a 14yr old grandson, are they in the form of individual vouchers that you have to carry around , are they a punch card. If lost can they be repalced? Can they be put on a lanyard
Your Disney Dining Plan (DDP) credits are tied to your Key to the World (KTTW) card. Your Key to the World card will be your room key, park ticket, AND your charging privileges (if you choose it to be) as well as your Disney Dining Plan card. Your DDP information will be electronically coded to the magnetic strip on the back, so it automatically deducts a credit when used, and your balance will be printed on your receipt. How nice is that?!
If your KTTW is lost, stolen, or misplaced, make sure you contact Guest Services or the Front Desk of your hotel so they can de-activate the card. Protect your card like you do your credit cards. Many people do put their cards on lanyards as well.
I hope this helps and have a MAGICAL vacation!

Good news! With
online check-in and an active
MagicBand, you can skip the front desk entirely if you wish and head straight to your Walt Disney World resort room.
When you complete online check-in, it will give you the option to get a text with your room number when it is available. You won't get the text until the day of your arrival, but I like getting that notification while we're on the road toward
Walt Disney World or, if we arrive early, while we're in the parks.
Once you reach your resort, you can stop at the desk for your welcome package. Or you can go straight to your room since your MagicBand also acts as your room key. (Any active MagicBand tied to your
My Disney Experience account should work.)
We love this benefit, especially for late arrival times.
Thanks for visiting the Disney Parks Moms Panel with your question. Your magical vacation is just around the corner. Enjoy every minute!

In my opinion, one of the best things about Walt Disney World is that there truly is something that appeals to every age group, from kids to kids-at-heart. I have a 12-year old and a 10-year old, and they both love
Disney's Animal Kingdom.
Since you asked specifically about entertainment offerings, I would recommend that you and your kids take a look at the Walt Disney World website to review the
list of entertainment for Disney's Animal Kingdom. By checking this out prior to your trip, you can see what your pre-teens might be interested in. I know both of my kids love the Festival of the Lion King show, and the Burudika street party is a lot of fun for all ages.
In addition to all of the entertainment offerings, Disney's Animal Kingdom has some amazing thrill-ride
attractions that are a big hit with pre-teen guests. My daughter's favorite Walt Disney World attraction, Expedition Everest - Legend of the Forbidden Mountain, can be found at Disney's Animal Kingdom. Also, I think your kids will be absolutely astounded when they visit Pandora - The World of Avatar. While there are no pirate or princess makeovers that take place at Disney's Animal Kingdom, you may want to visit the
Colors of Mo'ara Face Painting in Pandora - The World of Avatar.
I really think you and your pre-teens will love Disney's Animal Kingdom! See ya real soon!

We are going on the June 21st western caribbean cruise aboard the Fantasy, can you tell me what movies will be playing during our cruise? Thank you!
Welcome aboard, Megan! Are you getting excited about setting sail to the Western Caribbean this summer? The Disney Fantasy is a magnificent ship, so no matter which itinerary you'll sail, you're in for a real treat on that ship, but I do love that Western Caribbean itinerary!
Movie schedules are always subject to change, and since we're still a ways out from June, I do suggest you call the agents at Disney Cruise Line a week or two prior to sailing to get more specifics on the films on your sailing. However, I can tell you what is currently playing on the Fantasy and the new Disney films that are set to open as well.
Currently, the Fantasy is playing:
Finding Nemo 3D
The Lone Ranger
Thor: The Dark World
Saving Mr. Banks
Muppets Most Wanted
Need For Speed
Before you set sail, the following films will be premEARing on the Fantasy the same days they open in theaters back home:
Captain America: Winter Soldier - April 4
DisneyNature's Bears - April 18
Million Dollar Arm - May 16
Maleficent - May 30
I hope that gives you a glimpse at some of the great films you might see when you're onboard in just a few months, Megan! Stop back if you have other questions about your cruise before you set sail! I'm happy to help!

Hi there, Linda!
Welcome to the Disney Parks Moms Panel! Your Disney Cruise Line vacation will be here before you know it and I know how exciting it is that you can finally say, “I’m going on a Disney Cruise NEXT month!!” With a busy month ahead, I am sure it will fly by and before you know it, you will be off on an adventure you won’t soon forget.
While seeing all the incredible holiday decorations onboard would be such a treat, I am fairly certain that they will no longer be up during your sailing on January 4th. The voyage directly ahead of yours is not a Very Merrytime Sailing, so I am pretty sure that all the decorations will be taken down and away by the time you head out on your adventure. But, with so many incredible décor pieces onboard, you are certain to have some beautiful sights to see! In fact, my five-year old is amazed by the chandelier in the atrium onboard the
Disney Dream and has asked me multiple times if we can get the same one for our house! My response is always the same, “I think the atrium is a little bigger than our house…” But it really is beautiful as are all the other design elements onboard. And be sure you are looking for all those “hidden Mickeys”!
If seeing those holiday decorations are on your own bucket list, be sure to take advantage of the onboard future cruise booking specials and book a
Very Merrytime Sailing where you are guaranteed to see and enjoy them onboard!
Wishing you a magical and memorable adventure at sea!

Welcome aboard Ruth! Your Fantasy cruise is coming up very soon. I can't believe that it is almost November, can you? Your family has so much excitement in store on the Disney Fantasy, and I'm excited to hear that your kids are looking forward to Pirate Night.
Pirate Night is one of my family's favorite events on the cruises. And I have great news for you. Not only will your children get to see Captain Jack Sparrow in the Pirate Night show up on deck, but he is usually available for meet and greets earlier in the evening. On the Fantasy, his meet and greets are normally at Preludes in between dinner seatings (around 7:00 or so). Check your Personal Navigator for exact times on your sailing. Do plan to arrive early for this meet and greet. If you can be there half an hour early to get in line, you'll be all set, as this is a very popular meet and greet!
Then, head up on Decks 11 and 12 for the pirate partying to begin. My family has two favorite spots from which to watch the show on stage. First, if you can snag a spot on Deck 11 near the right of the stage by the steps up to Deck 12 that would be a great spot. It will be roped off, so if you can get up toward the front of the stage, you'll have a great view. Another great spot is on Deck 12, anywhere along the railing overlooking the stage area. There is a huge screen, called Funnel Vision, that shows the show and stage as well, so even if you're not right up front, you'll get to participate in the fun just as well.
I hope that helps as you get ready to count down these last few weeks until you set sail, Ruth! Have a wonderful cruise!

I am in the same boat, I would like to book for August this year. Upon calling the dining line, I have been told by several Cast Members that the window for booking the
Tomorrowland Terrace Fireworks Dessert Party opens 90 days in advance of your date. Even though you can view your date on the booking calendar online, it will show you that it is not available until 90 days out. Since we are in June, your 90 days should be creeping up soon. Just to be safe, I'd check every day before that as well just in case the window opens up early.
Have a magical day!

Hello Rebecca! I love that you're taking your niece for her first Walt Disney World vacation - it will be a time that she remembers for the rest of her life! Unfortunately, the Bibbidi Bobbidi Boutique is strictly for guests ages 3 -12 years old, so your niece would not be able to get a makeover there. However, there is another alternative that she may enjoy.
The Pirates League in Magic Kingdom does an Empress makeover or a Mermaid makeover, and is available to guests of all ages! Click the link I've provided, and you will find a full description and prices for each of these fun makeovers. Just like Bibbidi Bobbidi Boutique, you'll want to make advance reservations as early as possible, up to 180 days in advance of your arrival at a Walt Disney World resort. You can call 407-939-2739 for more information or reservations.
Be sure to pick up a complimentary 1st Visit button for your niece to wear throughout her Walt Disney World vacation. Guests wearing these buttons often get special greetings from Cast Members or characters, and it's sure to add a special touch to her vacation. Just stop by Guest Relations at the front of any of the theme parks to pick one up.
Rebecca, I hope this helps with your planning. Please don't hesitate to write me back if you have any other questions I can help you with. Have a magical day!

Hi Nancy! Thank you for bringing your question to planDisney!
I love that
Disneyland Resort has several
parking options for Guests. Each parking option has its own list of benefits, depending on your needs. If you're planning on using the Toy Story Parking Area on Harbor Blvd., you will have the option to take the shuttle bus or walk to or from the parks.
However, I strongly recommend taking the shuttle bus both ways. From my experience, the shuttle buses are frequent and provide a quick transportation option for Guests. Although walking is possible, depending on your speed, the one-mile walk could take about 20 minutes, give or take a few. With all the fun things to do at Disneyland Resort, like eating your way through the
California Adventure Food & Wine Festival, I think it could be a good idea to save your energy for strolling the resort.
Speaking of the
Food & Wine Festival, the festivities will be held from March 4 through April 26, 2022, and will be full of fantastic food, delicious beverages, and dance-worthy live music. I hope you have a chance to check it out.

Howdy, Amy! Thanks so much for stopping by with your question! All guests, no matter if they are staying at Disney's Fort Wilderness Resort & Campgrounds or not, are welcome to join in the fun of
Chip 'N Dale's Campfire Sing-A-Long. This is a great activity for the whole family, one that my daughters always enjoy.
The Chuck Wagon snack bar will be open nearby, where you can purchase kits to make yummy s'mores. If your schedule allows, do try to stay for Movies Under the Stars. This complimentary event is held on select nights and is also open to all guests. Closer to the time of your pixie-dusted adventures, you can
call ahead to check what movies are playing each night.
Be sure to allow plenty of time for travel to this event. When you arrive at Disney's Fort Wilderness Resort & Campgrounds, you will need to hop on one of the internal Yellow Loop buses to the Meadow Trading Post Area. The helpful driver will be happy to help you keep an eye out for this stop. Once you disembark, look across the street for the Meadow Recreation Area sign directing your to the Camp Fire Program.
I hope you have a magical day, Amy! Please let us know if you have any other questions.

Congratulations on securing a reservation at Cinderella's Royal Table! I was in a similar situation back in June. Be sure to take the credit card that you used to pay for this meal to your reservation. They will be able to refund your credit card and allow you to use your Dining Plan credits.

In general - when you have a reservation, how early before the scheduled time should you arrive and/or does it vary by restaurant? Also, what is the quickest route from POR to Chef Mickey's? Thanks so much!!
Welcome back to the Disney Parks Moms Panel. I generally like to arrive 10-15 minutes prior to our Advanced Dining Reservations. From Disney's Port Orleans - Riverside, you'll want to take a bus to Magic Kingdom and simply walk to Disney's Contemporary Resort. The monorail from Magic Kingdom will also transport you to Disney's Contemporary Resort, but since you mentioned the quickest route, I think walking from Magic Kingdom is the way to go! In not too long, you will be in
Walt Disney World ready to plummet down mountains, fly with Dumbo, and enjoy a delicious Dole Whip!
Looks like you will be visiting the mouse during a football fan's favorite time. Walt Disney World has not yet announced any special events for Super Bowl Sunday, but in past years many lounges and restaurants play the championship game for Guests to enjoy. Currently some more sports oriented venues-
Disney's All-Star Sports Resort and the
ESPN Club at Disney's BoardWalk-have not yet reopened, so your best bet is a resort lounge, your room, or a spot in
Disney Springs such as
Splitsville. This is a great day to visit the parks since so many fans will be watching the Super Bowl.
The Monday after the Super Bowl in the
Magic Kingdom is typically the sight of a
celebratory parade for the team winning the Vince Lombardi Trophy. The chance to see the victors ride down Main Street, U.S.A. is a thrill comparable to Belle discovering the Beast's library. This year's victory parade was cancelled due to the pandemic, so it is possible this could happen in 2022 as well.
Wishing you a wonderful visit to see Mickey!

Yeah! Your first trip to Walt Disney World! Prepare to have a blast zooming down Space Mountain, eating Mickey ice cream, and sailing through Captain Jack Sparrow's Caribbean.
With your kids' ages spanning over a decade, I suggest you try my family's favorite character meal, breakfast at Chef Mickey's. Mickey waffles with Mickey is a fabulous way to start the day. Another unique dining experience all three of you children would enjoy would be a meal at the 50's Prime Time Cafe in Disney's Hollywood Studios. Hilarious servers, a nostalgic atmosphere, TVs showing 50s sit coms while you eat at a linoleum table, and super swell milkshakes make this spot a family favorite.
Do try to stay at Disney's Art of Animation Resort. This shiny new, bold hotel has the beautiful Big Blue Pool, well themed family suites, and an excellent foods court. With a family suite, your family of five would have two bathrooms, a small kitchen to make a quick breakfast or snack in, a separate bedroom for the parents, and ample room.
Since you are visiting in June, a water park day would be a great big splash of fun for your family.
Disney's Typhoon Lagoon has Ketchakiddie Creek for your littlest princess, loads of waterslides for your bigger kids, raft rides for your whole family, and an awesome wave pool.
Planning your first visit to WDW can be overwhelming, so please contact me if you have additional questions about any aspect of your trip. Enjoy your very first experience with the magic of Disney!

Hi Erin, and welcome to planDisney!
Based on your question, it sounds like you are trying to buy last-minute tickets to the
Disneyland Resort during the holiday season. This time of year is a popular time to visit the Happiest Place on Earth, so be mindful that reservation availability will be limited. My best advice is to continue checking the
reservation availability calendar each day until the date you wish to visit. Sometimes reservations will open up last minute, even on the day of. If you see reservations open up, immediately visit the Disneyland Resort website to purchase your
tickets and secure a reservation.
Should availability open up for the days you wish to visit, note that the month of December is the holiday season at the Disneyland Resort. One show I always recommend to Guests during this time of year is
"Believe… in Holiday Magic" Fireworks at Disneyland Park. Viewing this firework spectacular is a once-in-a-lifetime experience, for sure!
Best of luck – I hope you are able to finalize your plans to visit the Disneyland Resort this year.

Welcome to planDisney, Karina!
Like you, I have a little princess of my own. How special you'll get to celebrate your daughter's birthday at The Most Magical Place on Earth! If she loves Disney Princesses, there are so many ways that you can make her birthday "practically perfect in every way."
I would suggest spending the day at Magic Kingdom Park, Karina. Your daughter's heart will skip a beat when she catches that first glimpse of
Cinderella Castle. You could even try and secure a dining reservation for
Cinderella's Royal Table. Although character dining is not currently offered at the legendary restaurant, it's still a magical, one-of-a-kind experience. Of course, she'll want to meet some real Disney Princesses. Therefore, you can take her to say hello to
Princess Tiana and a visiting Disney Princess at Princess Royal Hall. You can then find a viewing spot to watch the Disney Princesses promenade through the park in the
Festival of Fantasy Parade. If she wanted to become part of Ariel's world, she could ride
Under the Sea ~ Journey of The Little Mermaid.
If you wanted to add even more pixie dust to her day, you could make a reservation for the
Bibbidi Bobbidi Boutique at Magic Kingdom Park. With the help of Fairy Godmother's Apprentices, Guests ages 3 to 12 can be transformed into some of their favorite Disney characters. Reservations for this experience fill up fast, Karina! Therefore, you'll have to be quick. Reservations open 60 days in advance at 6:00 AM (ET).
I hope that's helpful, Karina. As you can see, she is certainly in store for the BEST. DAY. EVER! Thanks so much for stopping by planDisney.
See ya real soon!

Hiya, Ryan, and welcome to planDisney! Planning a Walt Disney World Resort vacation with a group is a wonderful experience, and me and my friends here at planDisney are here to help!
Ryan, it sounds like you're wanting to book a group vacation under your name, via the Walt Disney World Resort website. At this time, I'm unsure about whether your group is a family, a group of students, work colleagues, or another type of travel group. Happily, there are a few options to get you started.
Disney Destinations Gatherings website has helpful information about the types of events and groups that Walt Disney World can accommodate, and gives helpful insights about customizing those events for your particular group. Whether the event is for work training or a wedding celebration, there are many ways to host a group event at Walt Disney World Resort.
I chatted with a helpful Cast Member, and they let me know that you're always welcome to reach out to
Guest Services with any questions you have about booking any size Disney vacation.
Lastly, let me encourage you to become very familiar with the
My Disney Experience App, and especially the Family & Friends section, where you can link your account with others in your party, share plans, and make new plans together. My fellow planDisney panelist and friend Melanie wrote a great
how-to guide on linking people in your Family & Friends within the app, so give that a look!
Ryan, I hope I've been able to help you today with your question about booking a group vacation. If you have any more questions about planning your vacation, please stop back by planDisney anytime!
Have a magical day!

Welcome to planDisney and thank you for your question!
I’m not knowledgeable of any thresholds for group discounts, but whenever my family or friends are looking for the latest deals for the Walt Disney World Resort, I always tell them about the
Special Offers page! This has all the details on the deals pertaining to
Disney Resorts Collection hotel packages, which can include tickets.
Now when it comes to Florida Resident tickets only, you can find the details
here! Just make sure you’re signed into My Disney Experience before your purchase. Keep in mind that prices may vary and deals are subject to change. You can choose from one day Park Hopper tickets to multi-day ticket deals. When you’re purchasing through
My Disney Experience or the
My Disney Experience mobile app, you will need to
verify your residency. If you’re looking to book tickets in person, you can also show your Florida
identification to receive the special rate.
When you’re exploring your options, remember that after you book your 2023 tickets, you’ll also need to book
Disney Park Passes that go along with your tickets. At this time, both tickets and Disney Park Passes are needed for entry. You’ll just head to
this page, sign-in, and simply choose the Parks you want to go to for the days of your tickets!
I hope this “Bit By Bryanna” is useful for your next visit to the Walt Disney World Resort. If you have any other planning or vacation questions in the meantime,
please feel free to reach out, I’m always happy to help!
The Magic Is Calling,

As members of my own immediate family are eligible for the Blue Light Card, and also the Defence Discount Service, I'm absolutely delighted you've taken the time to bring me such an important question about their usage, Craig!
On your behalf, I've taken a quick peek at the current Blue Light Card - Terms and Conditions (which can be found by logging into your Blue Light Card account and searching for Walt Disney World Resort holidays).
With this information in mind Craig, I'm pleased to share that to the very best of my current knowledge, Blue Light Card discounts can be applied to
Magic Ticket-only bookings,
Power of Magic holiday packages,
Disney Resorts Collection hotel and ticket packages, and flight-inclusive holiday packages which are booked via the UK-based Walt Disney Travel Company.
To apply any currently available Blue Light Card or Defence Discount Service membership discounts to a booking made with the UK-based Walt Disney Travel Company; simply email your 8-digit booking reference to
firstname.lastname@example.org alongside the Blue Light Card discount code available at the date and time you made your original booking.
After which, helpful Cast Members will be more than happy to add any applicable discounts to your Disney holiday booking. Plus of course, Cast Members will then go ahead and issue an updated email invoice reflecting any applied discounts to your booking.
However, please do bear in mind that all
Special Offers, Deals, and Discounts at Walt Disney World Resort are always subject to change.
And lastly, I thought you may find these
Frequently Asked Questions - Blue Light Card to be a useful planning resource.
Craig, I sincerely hope I've been able to provide a little valuable insight and guidance for you today into your plans to holiday next
summer at Walt Disney World Resort. Please know you'll always have a friend in me here at planDisney!
Have a great Disney day ~
Your UK-based planDisney Panelist

We are interested in eating at the California Grill during our trip in October. If we can't get reservations, and eat at the lounge with the same menu at a first come basis-can we still come back for the fireworks viewing? Has anyone done this?
California Grill is a wonderful restaurant, one of my absolute favorites!
They have a beautiful view of the Magic Kingdom, especially at night when you can watch Wishes from above Cinderella Castle.
If you are unable to score an Advanced Dining Reservation (ADR) you can still enjoy menu offerings in the lounge, which has first-come, first-served seating. You most likely won't be able to get a spot here during the Magic Kingdom fireworks viewing because this is such a popular time, but you can most likely get a seat when the restaurant first opens, around 5:00 p.m.
This is a great way to enjoy the offerings at California Grill if you are unable to obtain an ADR.
Just go to the 2nd floor of the Contemporary Resort and visit the restaurants check-in desk. Inform them you are hoping for a seat in the open seating area and they will then escort you to the 15th floor via the special elevator.
